title: Are all 4-chromatic (2K2, K4)-free graphs recolourable?
micro_paper_assessment: Strong candidate. The present output now includes a genuine 6-vertex theorem slice plus a reusable structural reduction, but it is still not the title-theorem closure.
family_affinity: strong: last unresolved forbidden-pair recolourability case on four-vertex induced subgraphs
generalization_signal: moderate-positive: bichromatic chain-graph template scales, but the exact 6-vertex computation does not yet
candidate_theorem_slice: Up to isomorphism, the unique connected 6-vertex 4-chromatic (2K2, K4)-free graph is W6, and R5(W6) is connected; more generally, any frozen 5-colouring in the target class would force every bichromatic layer to be a chain graph of minimum degree at least 1 and would yield a triangle-free 2K2-free four-witness graph around a smallest colour class.
next_action: Attack the four-witness case split (C4/P4/K1,3) or extend the exact bounded check from 6 to 7 vertices.
